tip: Ask for ‘Kopi-C Siew Dai’ to impress locals with your kopi lingo.
Skip Starbucks — try a kopi peng (iced coffee) from a kopitiam.
Taste chilli crab with your hands — it’s messy but worth it.

Try economic rice (‘cai png’) and point at dishes like a pro.

Use 'lah', 'leh', and 'lor' to sound authentically Singaporean.
Say 'can!' to agree enthusiastically in true local fashion.
Catch the MRT during rush hour to experience the human sandwich.
Hold the rail, not your phone — MRT rides can get bumpy.
Reserve seats with tissue packs — locals call it ‘chope’ culture.
Address hawker stall uncles and aunties respectfully.
Don’t chew gum — it’s banned and a surefire tourist giveaway.
Try HDB rooftop gardens — hidden gems locals frequent.
Hike MacRitchie and spot cheeky monkeys (but don't feed them!).
Cycle from Marina Bay to East Coast Park for stunning sea views.
Eat durian at night like a true Singaporean — follow the smell.
Order ‘zero percent sugar’ bubble tea — sweet by default here!
Visit Little India on Sundays to see it at its liveliest.
Go to Geylang Serai during Ramadan bazaars — food heaven.
Explore hawker stalls late at night — many are open till 2am.
Take your trash with you — Singapore is known for cleanliness.
Use an EZ-Link card to tap on buses like a local ninja.
Try saying thank you in Mandarin, Malay, Tamil and Singlish.
Visit a void deck (open ground floor of flats) and observe daily life.
Use ‘lah’ to soften your tone — it works for any sentence.
Catch lion dances live during Chinese New Year at malls.
Visit a Taoist temple — light incense and observe respectfully.
Step into a mosque during visiting hours — attire may be provided.
Explore hidden alleys in Chinatown for murals and old shops.
Try Peranakan cuisine for a spicy, colorful cultural blend.
Buy a SIM card at the airport — 100GB for the price of kopi!
Drink from a plastic bag with a straw — very old-school local.
Eat carrot cake — surprise, it’s not a dessert!

Don’t jaywalk — hefty fines are a real thing.
Enjoy free Wi-Fi in public libraries and shopping malls.
Say ‘Uncle’ or ‘Auntie’ when calling older strangers — it's polite!
Attend a kopi appreciation class — yes, they exist!
Try prata flipping at an Indian food stall — they'll cheer you on.
Use your hands to eat banana leaf rice — just like locals do.
Watch open-air movie screenings at neighbourhood parks.

Visit a hawker centre during lunch rush for the full chaotic vibe.
Spot heartland birds — mynahs and hornbills are everywhere.
